name: Clinic Dentist
tagline: Full-stack management system for dental clinics
description: Private client project — a comprehensive web platform built to modernize the daily operations of a dental clinic. The system covers patient record management, appointment scheduling, treatment tracking, and operational reporting. Designed for multi-role access and server-validated workflows, it brings structure and efficiency to clinical workflows that were previously handled manually.

highlights.title: Patient Records
highlights.description: Centralized digital histories for each patient — treatments, diagnoses, uploaded documents, and visit notes — all in one searchable view.

highlights.title: Appointment Scheduling
highlights.description: Calendar-based booking system with conflict detection and automated status updates for each appointment.

highlights.title: Role-based Access
highlights.description: Distinct permission levels for admins, dentists, and receptionists — each role sees only the data and actions relevant to their workflow.

highlights.title: Operational Reporting
highlights.description: Dashboard with key clinical and operational metrics: daily appointment occupancy rate, total patient count, high-risk patient alerts based on odontogram findings, and a live view of today's schedule with per-appointment status.

challenges.title: Real-time appointment conflicts
challenges.problem: Multiple receptionists could simultaneously book the same time slot for a dentist, leading to double bookings that were only caught after confirmation.
challenges.solution: Implemented server-side conflict detection using time range intersection logic — a booking is rejected if any existing appointment for the same dentist satisfies inicio < newEnd AND fin > newStart, excluding cancelled appointments. The booking form also fetches occupied slots upfront and renders them as disabled in the UI, preventing most conflicts before submission.

challenges.title: Multi-role data isolation
challenges.problem: The application needed to serve admins, dentists, and receptionists with different views and permissions — without duplicating logic or creating separate apps.
challenges.solution: Built a single Next.js app with route-level and component-level guards driven by a role context stored in a profiles table linked to Supabase auth. Server components fetch only the data allowed for the current role, and Supabase RLS policies enforce the same rules at the database level.

challenges.title: Treatment history traceability
challenges.problem: Clinic staff needed a complete, immutable audit trail of every treatment applied to a patient — including who performed it and when — but the initial schema only stored the latest state.
challenges.solution: Redesigned the treatment schema with an append-only pattern: each clinical event is recorded as a new row in an evolutions table, storing the previous condition, the treatment applied, the affected tooth and surface, and a timestamp. The UI renders this as a chronological timeline per patient, giving staff a full traceable record of every intervention.
